status: Fixed in Team commit relevance gate lane
area: Team runtime commit ownership / closeout UX
finding: After all implementation and validation gates passed, governed commit was blocked because five stale or unrelated active Team runs required Coordinator/git.write ownership, even though the current framework claim belonged to the implementing actor. The recovery hint was an environment-prefixed native Git command rather than a governed ATM actor handoff.
expectedBehavior: A framework closeout should identify the owning Team runs, provide a governed actor handoff or cleanup command, and make the required commit route reproducible through ATM without asking an agent to impersonate another actor or manually translate an environment hint.
evidenceOrRepro: Fixed on 2026-07-11 by making Team git-owner enforcement apply only when staged paths intersect an active run's `file.write` lease. `node --strip-types scripts/validate-team-agents.ts --case hook-team-gate` verifies an implementer is still blocked for `packages/cli/src/commands/team.ts` but is allowed to commit unrelated `scripts/validate-team-agents.ts`.
followUp: Keep Team git.write ownership strict for matching file.write scopes and retain the focused unrelated-run regression so stale or unrelated runs cannot block framework maintenance.
